info.name: Hybrid LMS Backend — AUTH Module
info.description: Curated, representative Postman test suite for the AUTH module of the Hybrid LMS backend (Yazan Jawriyyeh — 174681). Covers every endpoint actually implemented as of this collection's creation date: Health Check, Registration, Email Verification, Guardian Approval, and Login. Requests marked [MANUAL] in their description require a value copied by a human from the running server's console (dev-mode email log) or a real inbox, because raw verification/approval tokens are, by design (DP-08), never returned in any API response. See README.md in this folder for full setup and CI/CD notes.

exec: // Collection-wide utility: a fresh unique email + matching NFR-01-compliant
// password for every request that needs brand-new, never-seen-before test data.
pm.test('data.timestamp is a valid ISO date', () => {

request.description: Liveness check only — does NOT verify DB/Redis connectivity. See README for why this matters when interpreting a green result.

exec: pm.test('requires_guardian_approval is NOT present for an adult', () => {

request.description: UC-AUTH-01 adult path. Uses a fresh random email/password generated by the collection-level pre-request script — safe to run repeatedly without collisions.
UC-AUTH-01 minor path — triggers UC-AUTH-02 (Guardian Approval) internally. birth_date is exactly 17 years and 364 days ago (still a minor), computed by the pre-request script.

item.name: Register — birth_date exactly 18 years ago (adult boundary)

exec: pm.test('status code is 201 (treated as adult)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Boundary test for ageCalculator.isMinor() — turning 18 exactly today must be classified as adult, matching the unit test already covering this in tests/unit/ageCalculator.test.js.

item.name: Register — repeat this request 6x via Collection Runner to trigger 429

exec: // On the 6th+ run within the window, this assertion is what should pass.
// On runs 1-5 it will correctly show as a 'failed' assertion — that is expected,
// not a bug. See README section 'Rate limiting test interpretation'.
pm.test('Retry-After header is a positive integer', () => {

request.description: IMPORTANT: uses a FIXED email (not the dynamic {{randomEmail}}) on purpose — the rate limiter keys off the identifier, so repeating this exact request is what accumulates hits. Run via Collection Runner with 6+ iterations, or Newman with -n 6. Because the backoff is escalating and persists 24h (violationsTtlSeconds), flush Redis between full test-suite runs — see README.

exec: pm.test.skip('manualVerificationToken environment variable is empty — see request description');

request.description: MANUAL STEP REQUIRED: run 'Register — Adult Student' above first, then copy the token from the '--- DEV EMAIL ---' block printed in the server's terminal (or the real inbox if Gmail is configured), paste it into the 'manualVerificationToken' environment variable, then run this request. This cannot be automated in Newman/CI without a dedicated test-only endpoint — see README.

exec: pm.test.skip('manualGuardianApprovalToken environment variable is empty — see request description');

request.description: MANUAL STEP REQUIRED: run 'Register — Minor Student' above, copy the approval_token from the server console's DEV EMAIL block (subject 'Guardian approval needed...'), paste into 'manualGuardianApprovalToken', then run this.

exec: pm.test('refresh_token cookie is set as HttpOnly', () => {

request.description: REQUIRES running scripts/seedTestUser.js once against your local database first — see README.

exec: pm.test('mfa_temp_token is present, access_token is NOT', () => {
pm.test('no refresh_token cookie is set before MFA is proven', () => {

request.description: Run this AND a request with a real email + wrong password, then diff the two bodies by hand once — they must be byte-identical (see tests/integration/login.test.js for the automated version of this exact check).

item.name: Setup TOTP — returns QR + manual key

request.description: manual_entry_key is the raw Base32 TOTP secret — copy it into an authenticator app (or a TOTP code generator) to compute a valid 6-digit code for 'Verify TOTP' below.

item.name: Verify TOTP — malformed code (not 6 digits) → 400 Zod
Verify TOTP — no pending setup → 400 NO_PENDING_SETUP

request.description: Fresh user who never called Setup TOTP first — use a NEW account for this specific test, not one that already ran 'Setup TOTP' above.

item.name: [MANUAL] Verify TOTP — valid code → enables MFA + backup codes

exec: pm.test.skip('manualTotpCode is empty — compute a live 6-digit code from manual_entry_key using any TOTP tool, paste into the environment variable, then run this within its 30s window');

request.description: MANUAL: run 'Setup TOTP' first, compute a live code from manual_entry_key (30-second window, ±1 step tolerance per SF-AUTH-02), set it as environment variable 'manualTotpCode', then run within that window.

item.name: Refresh — no cookies sent → 401 TOKEN_MISSING or 403 CSRF

request.description: requireCsrfToken runs BEFORE authService.refreshSession, so a request with zero cookies at all is caught by the CSRF layer first (403) — see tests/integration/refresh.test.js for the exact precedence.

item.name: Refresh — valid cookie jar (run right after Login) → new access_token

request.description: REQUIRES Postman's cookie jar to already hold refresh_token + csrf_token from a prior 'Login — Success' run in THE SAME session (enable 'Automatically follow redirects' + cookie persistence in Postman settings). Set {{csrfToken}} manually from the csrf_token cookie value shown in Postman's Cookies panel — Postman cannot read a non-HttpOnly cookie into a variable automatically without a small script; see README.

item.name: Forgot Password — non-existent email → SAME generic 200 (Enumeration prevention)

exec: pm.test('status code is 200 — byte-identical shape to the existing-email case', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: Reset Password — weak password → 400 before touching token (Zod-level)
[MANUAL] Reset Password — valid token → 200, all sessions terminated

exec: pm.test.skip('manualResetToken is empty — run "Forgot Password" above, copy the token from the DEV EMAIL console log, paste it here');

item.name: [MANUAL] GET /google — redirects to Google consent screen

request.description: MANUAL: Postman will follow the redirect automatically if 'Automatically follow redirects' is on — disable it for this specific test to inspect the 302 itself. The full consent→callback round trip requires a real browser and a real Google account; cannot be automated here.

item.name: Link Confirm — missing body fields → 400 Zod

request.description: KYC endpoints require an authenticated Bearer token AND, for review endpoints, an Admin/SuperAdmin role. Since /auth/register only creates Student/Instructor accounts, this collection relies on the SAME seeded test users used by 'Login — Success' above (seededLoginEmail/seededPassword), PLUS a seeded Admin account (seededAdminEmail/seededAdminPassword) that must exist in your local DB — see scripts/seedTestUser.js. Run 'Login as Student' and 'Login as Admin' below FIRST to populate the {{studentAccessToken}} and {{adminAccessToken}} collection variables used by every other request in this folder.

Requires a seeded Admin account with MFA already enabled (UC-AUTH-05 enforces MFA for Admin role) — if login returns mfa_required=true instead of access_token, this seeded account isn't configured for MFA-less testing. See README for the recommended seed script update.

MANUAL: attach two small PNG files locally to the 'id_document' and 'selfie' form fields before running (Postman does not let a collection JSON embed real binary files — see README). Any real PNG under 5MB works.

item.name: Submit — repeat 3x to observe rate limiting (kyc-submit bucket)

exec: // Threshold is env.rateLimit.maxAttempts (default 5) within the 10-min window.
// Run via Collection Runner 6+ times with the SAME studentAccessToken to trigger 429.

request.description: rateLimit('kyc-submit', by user id) — keyed off the Bearer token's user, so this must be run with the SAME studentAccessToken repeatedly, not a fresh login each time.
Proves the OWASP A01 fix (requireRole middleware) holds at the HTTP layer — see tests/integration/kycRoutes.test.js for the automated equivalent.

exec: pm.test.skip('lastKycRequestId is empty — submit a NEW request and re-run "List pending" first (previous request is already verified/closed)');

request.description: MANUAL PRE-STEP: submit a fresh KYC request (this one requires a request still in review_pending — run 'Submit — Student, valid PNG files' again, then 'List pending — Admin token' to refresh lastKycRequestId), since the previous test already closed the first request as 'verified'.

item.name: Reject — Student token attempts approve → 403 (state unchanged)
Security headers present on any response
